title: Combinational Circuits
summary: Multiplexers, decoders, adders, and comparators — outputs that depend only on present inputs.

sections.heading: Combinational vs Sequential
sections.body: A combinational circuit has no memory: outputs are a pure function of current inputs. There are no clocked storage elements. Any change at an input eventually propagates through the logic cone to outputs (after gate delays).
bullets: Described by Boolean equations or truth tables

sections.heading: Multiplexers
sections.body: An n-to-1 mux selects one of n data inputs using log₂(n) select lines. A 4:1 mux has 4 data inputs, 2 select bits, and 1 output. Muxes are universal for implementing any Boolean function.
bullets: Used in bus routing, LUTs in FPGAs, and function generators
Larger muxes built from 2:1 mux trees

sections.heading: Decoders and Encoders
sections.body: An n-to-2ⁿ decoder asserts exactly one of 2ⁿ outputs based on n input bits. Encoders perform the inverse — compress active inputs to a binary code. Priority encoders handle multiple simultaneous inputs.

callout.text: Decoder + OR gates = canonical way to implement any SOP function.

sections.heading: Adders and Arithmetic
sections.body: Half-adder computes sum and carry of two bits. Full-adder adds two bits plus carry-in. Ripple-carry adders chain full-adders; carry-lookahead adders reduce delay for wide operands.
bullets: Full-adder: 3 inputs (A, B, Cin), 2 outputs (Sum, Cout)
ALUs combine adders with logic for subtract, AND, OR, shift

sections.heading: Design Flow
sections.body: Typical combinational design: specify truth table → minimize (K-map, Quine-McCluskey, or synthesis) → implement with gates or RTL → verify timing (critical path) and functional correctness.

quiz.question: A combinational circuit's output depends on:
quiz.explanation: Combinational logic has no memory — output is f(inputs) only.

quiz.question: How many select lines for an 8:1 multiplexer?
quiz.explanation: log₂(8) = 3 select bits to choose among 8 inputs.

quiz.question: A 3-to-8 decoder has how many outputs?
Half-adder carry output is:
quiz.explanation: Carry out of half-adder is A AND B.

quiz.question: Critical path in combinational logic determines:
options: Maximum clock frequency if registered at outputs
quiz.explanation: Longest delay path limits how fast you can clock logic between registers.

quiz.question: Priority encoder differs from basic encoder because:
options: It resolves multiple active inputs by priority
quiz.explanation: Priority encoder outputs the code of the highest-priority active input.

flashcards.front: What is a LUT in FPGAs?
flashcards.back: Look-Up Table — small combinational function (often mux + SRAM)
Carry must propagate through all stages — slow for wide adds
Oscillation or glitches from feedback without registers
